summary: Auto-executes hardcoded AES-encrypted payload on require()
details: On require(), index.js invokes pkg.run() at module top level, which decrypts a hardcoded AES-GCM ciphertext (DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT in src/config/defaults.js) using a hardcoded passphrase ('default-dev-passphrase') and salt, pipes the result through two custom decoder layers from sibling packages 'encode-decode-codec' and 'position-unit-codec', and executes the decoded string via `new Function(runnable)()` in src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js:48 and:57. The executed bytes are opaque to static review and only materialize at import time on the installer's machine. Consuming this package as a library silently runs attacker-controlled code with no consent, no documented purpose, and no integrity check. The package's stated role as a 'codec pipeline' provides no legitimate reason to auto-evaluate a hidden encrypted blob; the codec dependencies are weaponized as decoder primitives to defeat static analysis. Empty README/author/keywords fields and the multi-package obfuscation chain corroborate intentional evasion.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
